perturbation.original_text: The Company will issue the Shares at a purchase price of $10.00 per share (the "Purchase Price"). The Company has filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "Commission") a registration statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333- ) (the "Registration Statement") containing a prospectus relating to the Offering for the registration of the Shares under the Securities Act of 1933 (the "1933 Act"), and has filed such amendments thereof and such amended prospectuses as may have been required to the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will issue the Shares at a purchase price of $10.00 per share (the "Purchase Price"). The Company has filed, or intends to file, with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "Commission") a registration statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333- ) (the "Registration Statement") containing a prospectus relating to the Offering for the registration of the Shares under the Securities Act of 1933 (the "1933 Act"), and may file such amendments thereof and such amended prospectuses as it deems necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the company 'has filed' a registration statement. The modified text introduces ambiguity by stating the company 'has filed, or intends to file' and 'may file such amendments thereof and such amended prospectuses as it deems necessary', implying that the company might not fully comply with SEC requirements regarding filing accurate and timely registration statements and amendments. This creates uncertainty and could contradict the mandatory nature of securities laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 5 of the Securities Act of 1933 makes it unlawful to sell securities using interstate commerce unless a registration statement is in effect. The modified text introduces uncertainty as to whether a registration statement is actually filed or if the company only intends to file, which contradicts the mandatory requirement of having an effective registration statement before offering securities.

Prohibitions relating to interstate commerce and the mails (a) Sale or delivery after sale of unregistered securities Unless a registration statement is in effect as to a security, it shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly- (1) to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce or
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity regarding the filing of a registration statement with the SEC. The original text confirms the filing, while the changed text uses 'has filed, or intends to file,' creating uncertainty. This contradicts Section 5 of the Securities Act of 1933, which, as supported by Scraped Law Snippet 1 (15 USC 77e), makes it 'unlawful for any person...to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce...unless a registration statement is in effect as to a security.' The phrase 'intends to file' suggests a potential offer of securities without a registration statement in effect, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 708,050 Shares within the period herein provided, this Agreement shall terminate and the Company shall refund to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares, the full amount which it may have received from them plus accrued interest as set forth in the Prospectus; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder, except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 6, 8 and 9 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 708,050 Shares within a reasonable period, this Agreement may terminate and the Company should consider refunding to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares, the amount it received from them plus possible accrued interest; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder, except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 6, 8 and 9 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces ambiguity by replacing 'within the period herein provided' with 'within a reasonable period', which is subjective. 'Shall refund' becomes 'should consider refunding' and 'full amount' is changed to 'amount', potentially creating disputes.  The removal of clarity on accrued interest also makes it uncertain whether the investors will be refunded fully.  This contradicts regulations concerning refunds to investors when minimum sales are not met.
perturbation.law_explanation: 17 CFR § 240.15c2-4 requires that funds received from investors in a contingency offering be promptly transmitted to a bank account or escrow agent and that those funds be promptly refunded if the contingency is not met. By using 'reasonable period', 'should consider refunding', and 'amount', the modified text introduces ambiguity about when and how refunds will be processed, contradicting the prompt refund requirements of the rule.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.15c2-4 - Transmission or maintenance of payments received in connection with underwritings. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.15c2-4 - Transmission or maintenance of payments received in connection with underwritings. CFR prev | next § 240.15c2-4 Transmission or maintenance of payments received in connection with underwritings. It shall constitute a “fraudulent, deceptive, or manipulative act or practice” as used in section 15(c)(2) of the Act, for any broker, dealer or municipal securities dealer participating in any distribution of securities, other than a firm-commitment underwriting , to accept any part of the sale price of any security being distributed unless: (a) The money or other consideration received is promptly transmitted to the persons entitled thereto; or (b) If the distribution is being made on an “all-or-none” basis, or on any other basis which contemplates that payment is not to be made to the person on whose behalf the distribution is being made until some further event or contingency occurs, (1) the money or other consideration received is promptly deposited in a separate bank account, as agent or trustee for the persons who have the beneficial interests therein, until the appropriate event or contingency has occurred, and then the funds are promptly transmitted or returned to the persons entitled thereto, or (2) all such funds are promptly transmitted to a bank which has agreed in writing to hold all such funds in escrow for the persons who have the beneficial interests therein and to transmit or return such funds directly to the persons entitled thereto when the appropriate event or contingency has occurred. [ 41 FR 22826 , June 7, 1976] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity regarding refunds, contradicting 17 CFR § 240.15c2-4 which mandates prompt transmission or return of funds under certain underwriting conditions. The original text clearly states 'shall refund' and specifies 'full amount' within 'the period herein provided.' The changed text softens this obligation to 'should consider refunding' and 'amount' within 'a reasonable period,' and removes clarity on accrued interest. This undermines the prompt refund requirements detailed in Scraped Law Snippet 2, which states funds must be 'promptly transmitted or returned to the persons entitled thereto' when a contingency occurs. The modifications introduce subjectivity and potential delays, failing to ensure the prompt and complete return of funds as legally required. This creates a legal contradiction because the modified text allows for discretionary action where the law requires prompt and decisive action to protect investors.

perturbation.original_text: The Company, the MHC and the Bank will use their best efforts to cause any post-effective amendment to the Registration Statement to be declared effective by the Commission and any post-effective amendment to the MHC Application to be approved by the OTS and will immediately upon receipt of any information concerning the events listed below notify the Agent:
perturbation.changed_text: The Company, the MHC and the Bank will consider using efforts to cause any post-effective amendment to the Registration Statement to be declared effective by the Commission and any post-effective amendment to the MHC Application to be approved by the OTS and will, when feasible, notify the Agent upon receipt of information about some events:
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'best efforts' with 'consider using efforts' and 'immediately notify' with 'when feasible, notify the Agent upon receipt of information about some events' introduces ambiguity and makes the obligations less stringent. This creates doubt about the timeliness and completeness of information provided to the Agent. This weakening of the obligation to provide prompt notification could conflict with securities laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: 17 CFR § 230.424 requires the filing of a prospectus that includes any substantive or material changes. The modified contract implies that notification, which would lead to prospectus changes, is not mandated, but based on 'some events', making compliance with the requirement to update and file the prospectus as and when there are material changes ambiguous.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 230.424 - Filing of prospectuses, number of copies. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 230.424 - Filing of prospectuses, number of copies. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 230.424 Filing of prospectuses, number of copies. (a) Except as provided in paragraph (f) of this section, five copies of every form of prospectus sent or given to any person prior to the effective date of the registration statement which varies from the form or forms of prospectus included in the registration statement as filed pursuant to § 230.402(a) of this chapter shall be filed as a part of the registration statement not later than the date such form of prospectus is first sent or given to any person: Provided, however, That only a form of prospectus that contains substantive changes from or additions to a prospectus previously filed with the Commission as part of a registration statement need be filed pursuant to this paragraph (a). (b) Ten copies of each form of prospectus purporting to comply with section 10 of the Act , except for documents constituting a prospectus pursuant to Rule 428(a) ( § 230.428(a) ) or free writing prospectuses pursuant to Rule 164 and Rule 433 ( § 230.164 and § 230.433 ), shall be filed with the Commission in the form in which it is used after the effectiveness of the registration statement and identified as required by paragraph (e) of this section; provided, however, that only a form of prospectus that contains substantive changes from or additions to a previously filed prospectus is required to be filed; Provided, further, that this paragraph (b) shall not apply in respect of a form of prospectus contained in a registration statement and relating solely to securities offered at competitive bidding, which prospectus is intended for use prior to the opening of bids. Ten copies of the form of prospectus shall be filed or transmitted for filing as f
perturbation.justification: The modification weakens obligations, creating ambiguity around compliance with securities laws. The original text uses 'best efforts' and 'immediately notify,' while the changed text uses 'consider using efforts' and 'when feasible, notify...about some events.' This creates an ambiguity that undermines the requirement to file a prospectus that includes any substantive or material changes under 17 CFR § 230.424, because the timing and content of the notification is now discretionary. The law explanation states this ambiguous notification could affect prospectus changes and compliance with updating and filing the prospectus when material changes occur. The changed text conflicts with the intent of 17 CFR § 230.424, as it makes the obligation to disclose material changes less stringent.
